tweet: @FCC @AjitPaiFCC If you happen to agree with me and think this sounds like a terrible idea, then now is the time to mobilize and fight back.

name: U.S. Senator Al Franken
tweet: @FCC @AjitPaiFCC The truth is there's no good reason to scrap #NetNeutrality, unless you want to give giant ISPs the power to establish fast and slow lanes.

tweet: @FCC @AjitPaiFCC Because massive corporations want it.

name: U.S. Senator Al Franken
tweet: @FCC @AjitPaiFCC So we know #NetNeutrality benefits consumers, businesses, entrepreneurs, and it promotes the free exchange of ideas on the internet.

tweet: @FCC @AjitPaiFCC @AjitPaiFCC claims we need to abandon strong #NetNeutrality protections because they are bad for broadband investment.

tweet: @FCC But @AjitPaiFCC, the head of the @FCC, is intent on eliminating the very protections that safeguard an open internet.

name: U.S. Senator Al Franken
tweet: @FCC #NetNeutrality allows a small business in Minnesota to compete with the big guys, it drives innovation, and it protects free speech.
